## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about testing your webinar registration page. So there's a challenge that comes with testing a webinar registration page, and that is testing requires traffic. And most webinar registration pages won't get enough traffic to make it seem worthwhile. So if you are going to run the webinar, you're going to email it out to your list, 300 total people are going to hit the page. And of those 100 people are going to register for the webinar, it seems on the surface like that is not worthwhile to test. But I do recommend that you should absolutely be testing, especially if you're going to run the webinar multiple times over time. If you're going to send add traffic to it, if you're going to turn your webinar into an evergreen webinar. So all three of these conditions are conditions that mean you're going to you're going to be exposing this webinar registration page to a lot of traffic. Also, one more thing time if when you should absolutely test it is if a lot of people, if over 500 people are going to see it, you should absolutely be testing. And so those are all conditions that I recommend testing under. But even if you don't meet those conditions, it is worthwhile to test at least one element, which is your headline of all of the elements on a registration page, the headline usually yields the biggest testing opportunity. And when I say testing opportunity, I mean, by making a change to the headline, you can totally change the amount of registration to your webinar. And so I recommend that even if you don't meet those criteria, but especially if you do, to test at least two different versions of your headline to attract attention. Now, in a minute, I'm going to talk about other elements that you should be testing on your page. But in the meantime, let's talk about that how you actually go about testing. So many page building programs now have testing built in, which is very cool. It used to be that we had to go pay 50 bucks a month to just for a tool that all it did was testing. Now that's included in the price with many of the web page building programs, just follow their instructions on how to do it. Now I'm going to give you generally. So generally, what you're going to do is you create a page, then you tell the program that you want to test it. And then you'll duplicate that page,

## [03:01 - 06:05]

make changes. And I recommend make one change to that new page and publish it within that test. And so then it tells the program, okay, these are the two different pages, two different versions of the same page that we want to show to audiences as they come to it. So then the program randomly chooses which page to show to visitors until it finds a winner. And then usually it just turns off the loser and only shows the winner to them so that you don't have to come back, make a decision, forget about it and continue a test for a long time when the program just solves that for you. Now, if your page builder doesn't have testing build in, I actually suggest changing page building programs because there are so many of them out there. There are a dime a dozen right now. And they're fairly similar in the quality of the pages that they produce. And so if yours doesn't do it, this is a key thing you want to have in that program. And so I'd seriously consider changing them, or just or maybe choosing another option is to choose a service that actually does do split testing, I recommend virtual website optimizer, which is at VWO.com. All right, let's talk about testing priorities on webinar registration pages. So these are the things that tend to create the highest amount of change by getting the right one in place. In other words, these are the places where running a test and getting a winner on that test can actually make a big difference in the amount of webinar sign up and attendance that you actually get. So first, as I said, is headline. Secondly, is the actual graphic design of the page. It is somewhat astounding to me how ugly many people's pages are. the mountains is directly the topic of your webinar. So you want that page to graphically look good, people's trust increases when they're on something that looks good.
